Introduction

The Greek Media database database provides an easy way to integrate the data with any external system. The API follows REST semantics, uses JSON to encode objects and relies on standard HTTP codes, machine and human readable errors to signal operation outcomes.

This documentation is generated automatically based on the tables and fields that are in your database. If you make changes to your database, table or fields it could be that the API interface has also changed. Therefore, make sure that you update your API implementation accordingly.

The ID of this database is: 408494
JavaScript example API client: axios
Python example API client: requests

Authentication

Baserow uses a simple token based authentication. You need to generate at least one database token in your settings to use the endpoints described below. It is possible to give create, read, update and delete permissions up until table level per token. You can authenticate to the API by providing your token in the HTTP authorization bearer token header. All API requests must be authenticated and made over HTTPS.

Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Airtable import report table

The ID of this table is: 913286

Fields

Each row in the Airtable import report table contains the following fields.

IDNameTypeCompatible filters
field_7919419Object namestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919420Scopenumbercontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordsingle_select_equalsingle_select_not_equalsingle_select_is_any_ofsingle_select_is_none_ofemptynot_empty
Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.

Accepts multi line text. If the rich text formatting is enabled, you can use markdown to format the text.

List fields

To list fields of the Airtable import report table a GET request has to be made to the Airtable import report fields endpoint. It's only possible to list the fields if the database token has read, create or update permissions.

Result field properties

  • id
    integer

    Field primary key. Can be used to generate the database column name by adding field_ prefix.

  • name
    string
    Field name.
  • table_id
    integer
    Related table id.
  • order
    integer
    Field order in table. 0 for the first field.
  • primary
    boolean

    Indicates if the field is a primary field. If true the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

  • type
    string
    Type defined for this field.
  • read_only
    boolean
    Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.
  • description
    string
    Field description

Some extra properties are not described here because they are type specific.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913286/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913286/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample

Copied!
[
    {
        "id": 7919419,
        "table_id": 913286,
        "name": "Object name",
        "order": 0,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": true,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919420,
        "table_id": 913286,
        "name": "Scope",
        "order": 1,
        "type": "single_select",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919421,
        "table_id": 913286,
        "name": "Table",
        "order": 2,
        "type": "single_select",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    }
]

List rows

To list rows in the Airtable import report table a GET request has to be made to the Airtable import report endpoint. The response is paginated and by default the first page is returned. The correct page can be fetched by providing the page and size query parameters.

Query parameters

  • page
    optional
    integer
    Default: 1
    Defines which page of rows should be returned.
  • size
    optional
    integer
    Default: 100
    Defines how many rows should be returned per page.
  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

    Additionally when user_field_names is set then the behaviour of the other GET parameters order_by, include and exclude changes. They instead expect comma separated lists of the actual field names instead.

  • search
    optional
    string
    Default: ''
    If provided only rows with data that matches the search query are going to be returned.
  • order_by
    optional
    string
    Default: 'id'

    Optionally the rows can be ordered by fields separated by comma. By default or if prepended with a '+' a field is ordered in ascending (A-Z) order, but by prepending the field with a '-' it can be ordered descending (Z-A).

    With user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of the field names to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=My Field,-My Field 2 the rows will ordered by the field called My Field in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by the field called My Field 2 in descending order.

    Ensure fields with names starting with a + or - are explicitly prepended with another + or -. E.g +-Name.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=field_1,-field_2 the rows will ordered by field_1 in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by field_2 in descending order.

  • filters
    optional
    JSON

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. This parameter accepts a JSON serialized string containing the filter tree to apply to this view. The filter tree is a nested structure containing the filters that need to be applied.

    With user_field_names:

    An example of a valid filter tree is the following: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": "Name", "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": 1, "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}

    Please note that if this parameter is provided, all other filter__{field}__{filter} will be ignored, as well as the filter_type parameter.

  • filter__{field}__{filter}
    optional
    string

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. Multiple filters may be applied if they follow the same format. The field and filter variables indicate how and where to apply the filter, respectively.

    With user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__Name__equal=test, only rows where the value of Name equals 'test' will be returned. This method is backwards compatible and will check against field_id if it fails on the name.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__field_1__equal=test, only rows where the value of field_1 equals 'test' will be returned.

    Please note that if the filters parameter is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

    A list of all filters can be found here.
  • filter_type
    optional
    string
    Default: 'AND'
    • AND: Indicates that the rows must match all the provided filters.
    • OR: Indicates that the rows only have to match one of the filters.

    This works only if two or more filters are provided.

  • include
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to include by providing the include query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example if you provide the following GET param: include=My Field,-My Field 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter include=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

  • exclude
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to exclude by providing the exclude query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field names to be excluded from the results. For example if you provide the following GET param: exclude=My Field,-My Field 2 then those fields will be excluded.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to exclude from the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter exclude=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 will be excluded.

  • view_id
    optional
    integer

    By default non of the filters and sorts outside of the ones defined in the query parameters are applied. You can add the filters and sorts of a view by providing its id in the view_id GET parameter. For example if you provide the following GET parameter view_id=1 then the filters and sorts defined in the view with id 1 will be applied. You can find the view_id in the context menu of any given view. It is the number in brackets behind the view name.

    With filter__{field}__{filter}

    Both the filter provided in the query parameter and the filters defined in the view will be applied.

    With order_by

    If order_by is provided then the sort defined in the view will be ignored.

  • {link_row_field}__join
    optional
    string

    Makes it possible to request a lookup of field values from a target table through existing link row fields. The parameter name has to be the name of an existing link row field, followed by __join. The value should be a list of field names for which we want to lookup additional values. You can provide one or multiple target fields. It is not possible to lookup a value of a link row field in the target table.

    With user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter LinkRowField__join=MyField,MyField2 then the values of MyField and My Field2 in the table linked by LinkRowField will be included in the response .

    Without user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter field_1__join=field_2,field_3 then the values of field_2 and field_3 in the table linked by field_1 will be included in the response.

Copy

GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913286/?user_field_names=true

}

Get row

Fetch a single Airtable import report row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that is requested.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913286/{row_id}/?user_field_names=true
Request sample

  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913286/{row_id}/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample

Copied!

field_7919420
field_7919421

    "id": 0,
    "order": "1.00000000000000000000",

        "id": 1,

        "value": "Option",
        "color": "light-blue"

    "Message": "string"
}

Create row

Create a new Airtable import report row.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before
    optional
    integer
    If provided then the newly created row will be positioned before the row with the provided id.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Object name field_7919419
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Scope field_7919420
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792766
    Field
    5792767
    Cell
    5792768
    View

    View sort

    View group by

    View color

    Interfaces
  • Table field_7919421
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792775

    optional

    5792781
    Unsupported feature
    5792782
    Data type mismatch
    5792783

  • Message field_7919423
    optional
    string
    Accepts multi line text. If the rich text formatting is enabled, you can use markdown to format the text.

Update row

Updates an existing Airtable import report row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be updated.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Move row

Moves an existing Airtable import report row before another row. If no before_id is provided, then the row will be moved to the end of the table.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    Moves the row related to the value.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before_id
    optional
    integer
    Moves the row related to the given `row_id` before the row related to the provided value. If not provided, then the row will be moved to the end.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
PATCH
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913286/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.patch(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913286/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
field_7919419
field_7919420
field_7919421
field_7919422

{
    "id": 0,
    "order": "1.00000000000000000000",

        "value": "Option",

    },

        "value": "Option",

        "value": "Option",

    },

}

Delete row

Deletes an existing Airtable import report row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be deleted.

Query parameters

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
DELETE
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913286/{row_id}/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.delete(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913286/{row_id}/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Media table

The ID of this table is: 913278

Fields

Each row in the Media table contains the following fields.

IDNameTypeCompatible filters
field_7919325Namestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919326Websitestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919330SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Dec2022decimalequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
Accepts a positive decimal with 3 decimal places after the dot.
field_7919342SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Jan2023decimalequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
Accepts a positive decimal with 3 decimal places after the dot.
field_7919343SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Feb2023decimalequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
Accepts a positive decimal with 3 decimal places after the dot.
field_7919338eMedia Directoryarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913281. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919339Geographical Levelnumbercontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordsingle_select_equalsingle_select_not_equalsingle_select_is_any_ofsingle_select_is_none_ofemptynot_empty
Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
5792696
Local
5792697
Regional
5792698
National
5792699
International
field_7919340Topicsarraycontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordmultiple_select_hasmultiple_select_has_notemptynot_empty
Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.

Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.

Accepts single line text.

Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913279. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Accepts a string that must be a URL.

Accepts a string that must be a URL.

Accepts a string that must be a URL.

Accepts a string that must be a URL.

Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919331Peoplearraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913280. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919341Articlesarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913283. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919344ECI Articlesarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913285. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

List fields

To list fields of the Media table a GET request has to be made to the Media fields endpoint. It's only possible to list the fields if the database token has read, create or update permissions.

Result field properties

  • id
    integer

    Field primary key. Can be used to generate the database column name by adding field_ prefix.

  • name
    string
    Field name.
  • table_id
    integer
    Related table id.
  • order
    integer
    Field order in table. 0 for the first field.
  • primary
    boolean

    Indicates if the field is a primary field. If true the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

  • type
    string
    Type defined for this field.
  • read_only
    boolean
    Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.
  • description
    string
    Field description

Some extra properties are not described here because they are type specific.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913278/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913278/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
[
    {
        "id": 7919325,
        "table_id": 913278,
        "name": "Name",
        "order": 0,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": true,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919326,
        "table_id": 913278,
        "name": "Website",
        "order": 1,
        "type": "url",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919330,
        "table_id": 913278,
        "name": "SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Dec2022",
        "order": 2,
        "type": "number",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    }
]

List rows

To list rows in the Media table a GET request has to be made to the Media endpoint. The response is paginated and by default the first page is returned. The correct page can be fetched by providing the page and size query parameters.

Query parameters

  • page
    optional
    integer
    Default: 1
    Defines which page of rows should be returned.
  • size
    optional
    integer
    Default: 100
    Defines how many rows should be returned per page.
  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

    Additionally when user_field_names is set then the behaviour of the other GET parameters order_by, include and exclude changes. They instead expect comma separated lists of the actual field names instead.

  • search
    optional
    string
    Default: ''
    If provided only rows with data that matches the search query are going to be returned.
  • order_by
    optional
    string
    Default: 'id'

    Optionally the rows can be ordered by fields separated by comma. By default or if prepended with a '+' a field is ordered in ascending (A-Z) order, but by prepending the field with a '-' it can be ordered descending (Z-A).

    With user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of the field names to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=My Field,-My Field 2 the rows will ordered by the field called My Field in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by the field called My Field 2 in descending order.

    Ensure fields with names starting with a + or - are explicitly prepended with another + or -. E.g +-Name.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=field_1,-field_2 the rows will ordered by field_1 in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by field_2 in descending order.

  • filters
    optional
    JSON

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. This parameter accepts a JSON serialized string containing the filter tree to apply to this view. The filter tree is a nested structure containing the filters that need to be applied.

    With user_field_names:

    An example of a valid filter tree is the following: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": "Name", "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": 1, "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}

    Please note that if this parameter is provided, all other filter__{field}__{filter} will be ignored, as well as the filter_type parameter.

  • filter__{field}__{filter}
    optional
    string

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. Multiple filters may be applied if they follow the same format. The field and filter variables indicate how and where to apply the filter, respectively.

    With user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__Name__equal=test, only rows where the value of Name equals 'test' will be returned. This method is backwards compatible and will check against field_id if it fails on the name.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__field_1__equal=test, only rows where the value of field_1 equals 'test' will be returned.

    Please note that if the filters parameter is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

    A list of all filters can be found here.
  • filter_type
    optional
    string
    Default: 'AND'
    • AND: Indicates that the rows must match all the provided filters.
    • OR: Indicates that the rows only have to match one of the filters.

    This works only if two or more filters are provided.

  • include
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to include by providing the include query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example if you provide the following GET param: include=My Field,-My Field 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter include=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

  • exclude
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to exclude by providing the exclude query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field names to be excluded from the results. For example if you provide the following GET param: exclude=My Field,-My Field 2 then those fields will be excluded.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to exclude from the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter exclude=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 will be excluded.

  • view_id
    optional
    integer

    By default non of the filters and sorts outside of the ones defined in the query parameters are applied. You can add the filters and sorts of a view by providing its id in the view_id GET parameter. For example if you provide the following GET parameter view_id=1 then the filters and sorts defined in the view with id 1 will be applied. You can find the view_id in the context menu of any given view. It is the number in brackets behind the view name.

    With filter__{field}__{filter}

    Both the filter provided in the query parameter and the filters defined in the view will be applied.

    With order_by

    If order_by is provided then the sort defined in the view will be ignored.

  • {link_row_field}__join
    optional
    string

    Makes it possible to request a lookup of field values from a target table through existing link row fields. The parameter name has to be the name of an existing link row field, followed by __join. The value should be a list of field names for which we want to lookup additional values. You can provide one or multiple target fields. It is not possible to lookup a value of a link row field in the target table.

    With user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter LinkRowField__join=MyField,MyField2 then the values of MyField and My Field2 in the table linked by LinkRowField will be included in the response .

    Without user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter field_1__join=field_2,field_3 then the values of field_2 and field_3 in the table linked by field_1 will be included in the response.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913278/?user_field_names=true

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913278/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Get row

Fetch a single Media row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that is requested.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

Create row

Create a new Media row.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before
    optional
    integer
    If provided then the newly created row will be positioned before the row with the provided id.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Name field_7919325
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Website field_7919326
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Dec2022 field_7919330
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a positive decimal with 3 decimal places after the dot.
  • SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Jan2023 field_7919342
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a positive decimal with 3 decimal places after the dot.
  • SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Feb2023 field_7919343
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a positive decimal with 3 decimal places after the dot.
  • eMedia Directory field_7919338
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913281. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Geographical Level field_7919339
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792696
    Local
    5792697
    Regional
    5792698
    National
    5792699
    International
  • Topics field_7919340
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.
    5792700
    Energy
    5792701
    Sports
    5792702
    Agricultural

  • Type of Media field_7919335
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.
    5792690

  • Description (Greek) field_7919333
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Media Companies field_7919334
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913279. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Twitter field_7919328

    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Instagram field_7919337

  • YouTube field_7919336
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • People field_7919331
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913280. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Articles field_7919341
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913283. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • ECI Articles field_7919344
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913285. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Update row

Updates an existing Media row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be updated.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Name field_7919325
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Website field_7919326
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Dec2022 field_7919330
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a positive decimal with 3 decimal places after the dot.
  • SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Jan2023 field_7919342
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a positive decimal with 3 decimal places after the dot.
  • SimilarWeb Total Visits (M) Feb2023 field_7919343
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a positive decimal with 3 decimal places after the dot.
  • eMedia Directory field_7919338
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913281. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Geographical Level field_7919339
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792696
    Local
    5792697
    Regional
    5792698
    National
    5792699
    International
  • Topics field_7919340
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.

If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Move row

Moves an existing Media row before another row. If no before_id is provided, then the row will be moved to the end of the table.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    Moves the row related to the value.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before_id
    optional
    integer
    Moves the row related to the given `row_id` before the row related to the provided value. If not provided, then the row will be moved to the end.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
PATCH
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913278/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true

  • JavaScript (axios)

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913278/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Delete row

Deletes an existing Media row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be deleted.

Query parameters

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
DELETE
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913278/{row_id}/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.delete(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913278/{row_id}/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Media Companies table

The ID of this table is: 913279

Fields

Each row in the Media Companies table contains the following fields.

IDNameTypeCompatible filters
field_7919345Namestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919346Mediaarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919347VIPstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919424Count (Media)numberequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
A read-only field connected to a link to table field which returns the number of relations.

List fields

To list fields of the Media Companies table a GET request has to be made to the Media Companies fields endpoint. It's only possible to list the fields if the database token has read, create or update permissions.

Result field properties

  • id
    integer

    Field primary key. Can be used to generate the database column name by adding field_ prefix.

  • name
    string
    Field name.
  • table_id
    integer
    Related table id.
  • order
    integer
    Field order in table. 0 for the first field.
  • primary
    boolean

    Indicates if the field is a primary field. If true the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

  • type
    string
    Type defined for this field.
  • read_only
    boolean
    Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.
  • description
    string
    Field description

Some extra properties are not described here because they are type specific.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913279/
Request sample

Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913279/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
[
    {
        "id": 7919345,
        "table_id": 913279,
        "name": "Name",
        "order": 0,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": true,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919346,
        "table_id": 913279,
        "name": "Media",
        "order": 1,
        "type": "link_row",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919347,
        "table_id": 913279,
        "name": "VIP",
        "order": 2,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    }
]

List rows

To list rows in the Media Companies table a GET request has to be made to the Media Companies endpoint. The response is paginated and by default the first page is returned. The correct page can be fetched by providing the page and size query parameters.

Query parameters

  • page
    optional
    integer
    Default: 1
    Defines which page of rows should be returned.
  • size
    optional
    integer
    Default: 100
    Defines how many rows should be returned per page.
  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

    Additionally when user_field_names is set then the behaviour of the other GET parameters order_by, include and exclude changes. They instead expect comma separated lists of the actual field names instead.

  • search
    optional
    string
    Default: ''
    If provided only rows with data that matches the search query are going to be returned.
  • order_by
    optional
    string
    Default: 'id'

    Optionally the rows can be ordered by fields separated by comma. By default or if prepended with a '+' a field is ordered in ascending (A-Z) order, but by prepending the field with a '-' it can be ordered descending (Z-A).

    With user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of the field names to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=My Field,-My Field 2 the rows will ordered by the field called My Field in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by the field called My Field 2 in descending order.

    Ensure fields with names starting with a + or - are explicitly prepended with another + or -. E.g +-Name.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=field_1,-field_2 the rows will ordered by field_1 in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by field_2 in descending order.

  • filters
    optional
    JSON

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. This parameter accepts a JSON serialized string containing the filter tree to apply to this view. The filter tree is a nested structure containing the filters that need to be applied.

    With user_field_names:

    An example of a valid filter tree is the following: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": "Name", "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": 1, "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}

    Please note that if this parameter is provided, all other filter__{field}__{filter} will be ignored, as well as the filter_type parameter.

  • filter__{field}__{filter}
    optional
    string

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. Multiple filters may be applied if they follow the same format. The field and filter variables indicate how and where to apply the filter, respectively.

    With user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__Name__equal=test, only rows where the value of Name equals 'test' will be returned. This method is backwards compatible and will check against field_id if it fails on the name.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__field_1__equal=test, only rows where the value of field_1 equals 'test' will be returned.

    Please note that if the filters parameter is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

    A list of all filters can be found here.
  • filter_type
    optional
    string
    Default: 'AND'
    • AND: Indicates that the rows must match all the provided filters.
    • OR: Indicates that the rows only have to match one of the filters.

    This works only if two or more filters are provided.

  • include
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to include by providing the include query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example if you provide the following GET param: include=My Field,-My Field 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter include=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

  • exclude
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to exclude by providing the exclude query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field names to be excluded from the results. For example if you provide the following GET param: exclude=My Field,-My Field 2 then those fields will be excluded.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to exclude from the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter exclude=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 will be excluded.

  • view_id
    optional
    integer

    By default non of the filters and sorts outside of the ones defined in the query parameters are applied. You can add the filters and sorts of a view by providing its id in the view_id GET parameter. For example if you provide the following GET parameter view_id=1 then the filters and sorts defined in the view with id 1 will be applied. You can find the view_id in the context menu of any given view. It is the number in brackets behind the view name.

    With filter__{field}__{filter}

    Both the filter provided in the query parameter and the filters defined in the view will be applied.

    With order_by

    If order_by is provided then the sort defined in the view will be ignored.

  • {link_row_field}__join
    optional
    string

    Makes it possible to request a lookup of field values from a target table through existing link row fields. The parameter name has to be the name of an existing link row field, followed by __join. The value should be a list of field names for which we want to lookup additional values. You can provide one or multiple target fields. It is not possible to lookup a value of a link row field in the target table.

    With user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter LinkRowField__join=MyField,MyField2 then the values of MyField and My Field2 in the table linked by LinkRowField will be included in the response .

    Without user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter field_1__join=field_2,field_3 then the values of field_2 and field_3 in the table linked by field_1 will be included in the response.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913279/?user_field_names=true

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913279/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={

)

Get row

Fetch a single Media Companies row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that is requested.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

Create row

Create a new Media Companies row.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before
    optional
    integer
    If provided then the newly created row will be positioned before the row with the provided id.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Name field_7919345
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Media field_7919346
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • VIP field_7919347
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.

Copied!

https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913279/?user_field_names=true

field_7919345

    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    },
    json={
        "Name": "string",
        "Media": [
            1
        ],
        "VIP": "string"
    }
)

        }
    ],
    "VIP": "string",
    "Count (Media)": 0
}

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

  • Media field_7919346

    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Move row

Moves an existing Media Companies row before another row. If no before_id is provided, then the row will be moved to the end of the table.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    Moves the row related to the value.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before_id
    optional
    integer
    Moves the row related to the given `row_id` before the row related to the provided value. If not provided, then the row will be moved to the end.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copied!

    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Journalists table

The ID of this table is: 913280

Fields

Each row in the Journalists table contains the following fields.

IDNameTypeCompatible filters
field_7919348Namestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919350Mediaarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919349Emailstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be an email address.
field_7919351Link on Mediastringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919352Primary Beatarraycontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordmultiple_select_hasmultiple_select_has_notemptynot_empty
Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.

Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913283. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919354Notesstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919355Twitterstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919358LinkedInstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919356Statusnumbercontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordsingle_select_equalsingle_select_not_equalsingle_select_is_any_ofsingle_select_is_none_ofemptynot_empty
Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.

Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.

List fields

To list fields of the Journalists table a GET request has to be made to the Journalists fields endpoint. It's only possible to list the fields if the database token has read, create or update permissions.

Result field properties

  • id
    integer

    Field primary key. Can be used to generate the database column name by adding field_ prefix.

  • name
    string
    Field name.
  • table_id
    integer
    Related table id.
  • order
    integer
    Field order in table. 0 for the first field.
  • primary
    boolean

    Indicates if the field is a primary field. If true the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

  • type
    string
    Type defined for this field.
  • read_only
    boolean
    Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.
  • description
    string
    Field description

Some extra properties are not described here because they are type specific.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913280/
Request sample

Copied!

  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913280/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample

Copied!

    {
        "id": 7919348,
        "table_id": 913280,
        "name": "Name",
        "order": 0,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": true,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919350,
        "table_id": 913280,
        "name": "Media",
        "order": 1,
        "type": "link_row",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919349,
        "table_id": 913280,
        "name": "Email",
        "order": 2,
        "type": "email",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    }
]

List rows

To list rows in the Journalists table a GET request has to be made to the Journalists endpoint. The response is paginated and by default the first page is returned. The correct page can be fetched by providing the page and size query parameters.

Query parameters

  • page
    optional
    integer
    Default: 1
    Defines which page of rows should be returned.
  • size
    optional
    integer
    Default: 100
    Defines how many rows should be returned per page.
  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

    Additionally when user_field_names is set then the behaviour of the other GET parameters order_by, include and exclude changes. They instead expect comma separated lists of the actual field names instead.

  • search
    optional
    string
    Default: ''
    If provided only rows with data that matches the search query are going to be returned.
  • order_by
    optional
    string
    Default: 'id'

    Optionally the rows can be ordered by fields separated by comma. By default or if prepended with a '+' a field is ordered in ascending (A-Z) order, but by prepending the field with a '-' it can be ordered descending (Z-A).

    With user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of the field names to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=My Field,-My Field 2 the rows will ordered by the field called My Field in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by the field called My Field 2 in descending order.

    Ensure fields with names starting with a + or - are explicitly prepended with another + or -. E.g +-Name.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=field_1,-field_2 the rows will ordered by field_1 in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by field_2 in descending order.

  • filters
    optional
    JSON

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. This parameter accepts a JSON serialized string containing the filter tree to apply to this view. The filter tree is a nested structure containing the filters that need to be applied.

    With user_field_names:

    An example of a valid filter tree is the following: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": "Name", "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": 1, "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}

    Please note that if this parameter is provided, all other filter__{field}__{filter} will be ignored, as well as the filter_type parameter.

  • filter__{field}__{filter}
    optional
    string

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. Multiple filters may be applied if they follow the same format. The field and filter variables indicate how and where to apply the filter, respectively.

    With user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__Name__equal=test, only rows where the value of Name equals 'test' will be returned. This method is backwards compatible and will check against field_id if it fails on the name.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__field_1__equal=test, only rows where the value of field_1 equals 'test' will be returned.

    Please note that if the filters parameter is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

    A list of all filters can be found here.
  • filter_type
    optional
    string
    Default: 'AND'
    • AND: Indicates that the rows must match all the provided filters.
    • OR: Indicates that the rows only have to match one of the filters.

    This works only if two or more filters are provided.

  • include
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to include by providing the include query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example if you provide the following GET param: include=My Field,-My Field 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter include=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

  • exclude
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to exclude by providing the exclude query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field names to be excluded from the results. For example if you provide the following GET param: exclude=My Field,-My Field 2 then those fields will be excluded.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to exclude from the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter exclude=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 will be excluded.

  • view_id
    optional
    integer

    By default non of the filters and sorts outside of the ones defined in the query parameters are applied. You can add the filters and sorts of a view by providing its id in the view_id GET parameter. For example if you provide the following GET parameter view_id=1 then the filters and sorts defined in the view with id 1 will be applied. You can find the view_id in the context menu of any given view. It is the number in brackets behind the view name.

    With filter__{field}__{filter}

    Both the filter provided in the query parameter and the filters defined in the view will be applied.

    With order_by

    If order_by is provided then the sort defined in the view will be ignored.

  • {link_row_field}__join
    optional
    string

    Makes it possible to request a lookup of field values from a target table through existing link row fields. The parameter name has to be the name of an existing link row field, followed by __join. The value should be a list of field names for which we want to lookup additional values. You can provide one or multiple target fields. It is not possible to lookup a value of a link row field in the target table.

    With user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter LinkRowField__join=MyField,MyField2 then the values of MyField and My Field2 in the table linked by LinkRowField will be included in the response .

    Without user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter field_1__join=field_2,field_3 then the values of field_2 and field_3 in the table linked by field_1 will be included in the response.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913280/?user_field_names=true

)
