Get row

Fetch a single Journalists row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that is requested.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

)

Create row

Create a new Journalists row.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before
    optional
    integer
    If provided then the newly created row will be positioned before the row with the provided id.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Name field_7919348
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Media field_7919350
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Email field_7919349
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be an email address.
  • Link on Media field_7919351
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Primary Beat field_7919352
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.
    5792712
    Politics
    5792713
    Financial
    5792714
    Energy
    5792715
    Culture
    5792716
    Environmental
    5792717
    Sports
    5792718
    * Newsfeed
    5792719
    FMCG
    5792720
    Technology
    5792721
    Blockchain
    5792722
    Education
    5792723
    Health
  • Articles field_7919353
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913283. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Notes field_7919354
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Twitter field_7919355
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • LinkedIn field_7919358
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Status field_7919356
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792724
    Lead
    5792727
    Contacted
    5792728
    Active
    5792726
    Unreachable
    5792725
    Not a great fit
    5792729
    Unavailable
  • Channel field_7919357
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792730
    Email
    5792731
    LinkedIn
    5792732
    Contact Form

Update row

Updates an existing Journalists row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be updated.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Name field_7919348
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Media field_7919350
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Email field_7919349
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be an email address.
  • Link on Media field_7919351
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Primary Beat field_7919352
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.

Move row

Moves an existing Journalists row before another row. If no before_id is provided, then the row will be moved to the end of the table.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    Moves the row related to the value.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before_id
    optional
    integer
    Moves the row related to the given `row_id` before the row related to the provided value. If not provided, then the row will be moved to the end.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
PATCH
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913280/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true

    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Delete row

Deletes an existing Journalists row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be deleted.

Query parameters

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copied!

  • cURL

    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

eMedia directory table

The ID of this table is: 913281

Fields

Each row in the eMedia directory table contains the following fields.

IDNameTypeCompatible filters
field_7919359IDstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919360Mediaarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

List fields

To list fields of the eMedia directory table a GET request has to be made to the eMedia directory fields endpoint. It's only possible to list the fields if the database token has read, create or update permissions.

Result field properties

  • id
    integer

    Field primary key. Can be used to generate the database column name by adding field_ prefix.

  • name
    string
    Field name.
  • table_id
    integer
    Related table id.
  • order
    integer
    Field order in table. 0 for the first field.
  • primary
    boolean

    Indicates if the field is a primary field. If true the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

  • type
    string
    Type defined for this field.
  • read_only
    boolean
    Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.
  • description
    string
    Field description

Some extra properties are not described here because they are type specific.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913281/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913281/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
[
    {
        "id": 7919359,
        "table_id": 913281,
        "name": "ID",
        "order": 0,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": true,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919360,
        "table_id": 913281,
        "name": "Media",
        "order": 1,
        "type": "link_row",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    }
]

List rows

To list rows in the eMedia directory table a GET request has to be made to the eMedia directory endpoint. The response is paginated and by default the first page is returned. The correct page can be fetched by providing the page and size query parameters.

Query parameters

  • page
    optional
    integer
    Default: 1
    Defines which page of rows should be returned.
  • size
    optional
    integer
    Default: 100
    Defines how many rows should be returned per page.
  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

    Additionally when user_field_names is set then the behaviour of the other GET parameters order_by, include and exclude changes. They instead expect comma separated lists of the actual field names instead.

  • search
    optional
    string
    Default: ''
    If provided only rows with data that matches the search query are going to be returned.
  • order_by
    optional
    string
    Default: 'id'

    Optionally the rows can be ordered by fields separated by comma. By default or if prepended with a '+' a field is ordered in ascending (A-Z) order, but by prepending the field with a '-' it can be ordered descending (Z-A).

    With user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of the field names to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=My Field,-My Field 2 the rows will ordered by the field called My Field in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by the field called My Field 2 in descending order.

    Ensure fields with names starting with a + or - are explicitly prepended with another + or -. E.g +-Name.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=field_1,-field_2 the rows will ordered by field_1 in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by field_2 in descending order.

  • filters
    optional
    JSON

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. This parameter accepts a JSON serialized string containing the filter tree to apply to this view. The filter tree is a nested structure containing the filters that need to be applied.

    With user_field_names:

    An example of a valid filter tree is the following: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": "Name", "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": 1, "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}

    Please note that if this parameter is provided, all other filter__{field}__{filter} will be ignored, as well as the filter_type parameter.

  • filter__{field}__{filter}
    optional
    string

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. Multiple filters may be applied if they follow the same format. The field and filter variables indicate how and where to apply the filter, respectively.

    With user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__Name__equal=test, only rows where the value of Name equals 'test' will be returned. This method is backwards compatible and will check against field_id if it fails on the name.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__field_1__equal=test, only rows where the value of field_1 equals 'test' will be returned.

    Please note that if the filters parameter is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

    A list of all filters can be found here.
  • filter_type
    optional
    string
    Default: 'AND'
    • AND: Indicates that the rows must match all the provided filters.
    • OR: Indicates that the rows only have to match one of the filters.

    This works only if two or more filters are provided.

  • include
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to include by providing the include query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example if you provide the following GET param: include=My Field,-My Field 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter include=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

  • exclude
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to exclude by providing the exclude query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field names to be excluded from the results. For example if you provide the following GET param: exclude=My Field,-My Field 2 then those fields will be excluded.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to exclude from the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter exclude=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 will be excluded.

  • view_id
    optional
    integer

    By default non of the filters and sorts outside of the ones defined in the query parameters are applied. You can add the filters and sorts of a view by providing its id in the view_id GET parameter. For example if you provide the following GET parameter view_id=1 then the filters and sorts defined in the view with id 1 will be applied. You can find the view_id in the context menu of any given view. It is the number in brackets behind the view name.

    With filter__{field}__{filter}

    Both the filter provided in the query parameter and the filters defined in the view will be applied.

    With order_by

    If order_by is provided then the sort defined in the view will be ignored.

  • {link_row_field}__join
    optional
    string

    Makes it possible to request a lookup of field values from a target table through existing link row fields. The parameter name has to be the name of an existing link row field, followed by __join. The value should be a list of field names for which we want to lookup additional values. You can provide one or multiple target fields. It is not possible to lookup a value of a link row field in the target table.

    With user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter LinkRowField__join=MyField,MyField2 then the values of MyField and My Field2 in the table linked by LinkRowField will be included in the response .

    Without user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter field_1__join=field_2,field_3 then the values of field_2 and field_3 in the table linked by field_1 will be included in the response.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913281/?user_field_names=true
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913281/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
field_7919359
field_7919360
{
    "count": 1024,
    "next": "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913281/?page=2",
    "previous": null,
    "results": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "order": "1.00000000000000000000",
            "ID": "string",
            "Media": [
                {
                    "id": 0,
                    "value": "string"
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}

Get row

Fetch a single eMedia directory row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that is requested.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913281/{row_id}/?user_field_names=true
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913281/{row_id}/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
field_7919359
field_7919360
{
    "id": 0,
    "order": "1.00000000000000000000",
    "ID": "string",
    "Media": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "value": "string"
        }
    ]
}

Create row

Create a new eMedia directory row.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before
    optional
    integer
    If provided then the newly created row will be positioned before the row with the provided id.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • ID field_7919359
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Media field_7919360
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
Copy
Copied!
POST
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913281/?user_field_names=true
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
field_7919359
field_7919360
requests.post(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913281/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    },
    json={
        "ID": "string",
        "Media": [
            1
        ]
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
field_7919359
field_7919360
{
    "id": 0,
    "order": "1.00000000000000000000",
    "ID": "string",
    "Media": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "value": "string"
        }
    ]
}

Update row

Updates an existing eMedia directory row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be updated.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • ID field_7919359
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Media field_7919360
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Move row

Moves an existing eMedia directory row before another row. If no before_id is provided, then the row will be moved to the end of the table.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    Moves the row related to the value.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before_id
    optional
    integer
    Moves the row related to the given `row_id` before the row related to the provided value. If not provided, then the row will be moved to the end.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
PATCH
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913281/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true
Request sample

  • JavaScript (axios)

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913281/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample

Copied!

field_7919360
{
    "id": 0,
    "order": "1.00000000000000000000",
    "ID": "string",
    "Media": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "value": "string"
        }
    ]
}

Query parameters

    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Creators table

The ID of this table is: 913282

Fields

Each row in the Creators table contains the following fields.

IDNameTypeCompatible filters
field_7919361Namestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919373Topicarraycontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordmultiple_select_hasmultiple_select_has_notemptynot_empty
Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.

5792735
Tech
field_7919378Real Namestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919375Emailstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be an email address.
field_7919364YouTubestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919367followers-YTdecimalequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
Accepts a positive decimal with 1 decimal places after the dot.
field_7919377Statusnumbercontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordsingle_select_equalsingle_select_not_equalsingle_select_is_any_ofsingle_select_is_none_ofemptynot_empty
Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.

Not suggested

Accepts a string that must be a URL.

Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919366followers-Instanumberequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
Accepts a positive number.
field_7919362Facebookstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919365followers-FBnumberequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
Accepts a positive number.
field_7919368views-YTnumberequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
Accepts a positive number.
field_7919369TikTokstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919370followers-TTnumberequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
Accepts a positive number.
field_7919371Twitterstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919372followers-TWnumberequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
Accepts a positive number.
field_7919376Articlesarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913283. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

List fields

To list fields of the Creators table a GET request has to be made to the Creators fields endpoint. It's only possible to list the fields if the database token has read, create or update permissions.

Result field properties

  • id
    integer

    Field primary key. Can be used to generate the database column name by adding field_ prefix.

  • name
    string
    Field name.
  • table_id
    integer
    Related table id.
  • order
    integer
    Field order in table. 0 for the first field.
  • primary
    boolean

    Indicates if the field is a primary field. If true the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

  • type
    string
    Type defined for this field.
  • read_only
    boolean
    Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.
  • description
    string
    Field description

Some extra properties are not described here because they are type specific.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913282/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913282/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
[
    {
        "id": 7919361,
        "table_id": 913282,
        "name": "Name",
        "order": 0,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": true,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919373,
        "table_id": 913282,
        "name": "Topic",
        "order": 1,
        "type": "multiple_select",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919378,
        "table_id": 913282,
        "name": "Real Name",
        "order": 2,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    }
]

List rows

To list rows in the Creators table a GET request has to be made to the Creators endpoint. The response is paginated and by default the first page is returned. The correct page can be fetched by providing the page and size query parameters.

Query parameters

  • page
    optional
    integer
    Default: 1
    Defines which page of rows should be returned.
  • size
    optional
    integer
    Default: 100
    Defines how many rows should be returned per page.
  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

    Additionally when user_field_names is set then the behaviour of the other GET parameters order_by, include and exclude changes. They instead expect comma separated lists of the actual field names instead.

  • search
    optional
    string
    Default: ''
    If provided only rows with data that matches the search query are going to be returned.
  • order_by
    optional
    string
    Default: 'id'

    Optionally the rows can be ordered by fields separated by comma. By default or if prepended with a '+' a field is ordered in ascending (A-Z) order, but by prepending the field with a '-' it can be ordered descending (Z-A).

    With user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of the field names to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=My Field,-My Field 2 the rows will ordered by the field called My Field in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by the field called My Field 2 in descending order.

    Ensure fields with names starting with a + or - are explicitly prepended with another + or -. E.g +-Name.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=field_1,-field_2 the rows will ordered by field_1 in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by field_2 in descending order.

  • filters
    optional
    JSON

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. This parameter accepts a JSON serialized string containing the filter tree to apply to this view. The filter tree is a nested structure containing the filters that need to be applied.

    With user_field_names:

    An example of a valid filter tree is the following: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": "Name", "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": 1, "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}

    Please note that if this parameter is provided, all other filter__{field}__{filter} will be ignored, as well as the filter_type parameter.

  • filter__{field}__{filter}
    optional
    string

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. Multiple filters may be applied if they follow the same format. The field and filter variables indicate how and where to apply the filter, respectively.

    With user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__Name__equal=test, only rows where the value of Name equals 'test' will be returned. This method is backwards compatible and will check against field_id if it fails on the name.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__field_1__equal=test, only rows where the value of field_1 equals 'test' will be returned.

    Please note that if the filters parameter is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

    A list of all filters can be found here.
  • filter_type
    optional
    string
    Default: 'AND'
    • AND: Indicates that the rows must match all the provided filters.
    • OR: Indicates that the rows only have to match one of the filters.

    This works only if two or more filters are provided.

  • include
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to include by providing the include query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example if you provide the following GET param: include=My Field,-My Field 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter include=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

  • exclude
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to exclude by providing the exclude query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field names to be excluded from the results. For example if you provide the following GET param: exclude=My Field,-My Field 2 then those fields will be excluded.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to exclude from the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter exclude=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 will be excluded.

  • view_id
    optional
    integer

    By default non of the filters and sorts outside of the ones defined in the query parameters are applied. You can add the filters and sorts of a view by providing its id in the view_id GET parameter. For example if you provide the following GET parameter view_id=1 then the filters and sorts defined in the view with id 1 will be applied. You can find the view_id in the context menu of any given view. It is the number in brackets behind the view name.

    With filter__{field}__{filter}

    Both the filter provided in the query parameter and the filters defined in the view will be applied.

    With order_by

    If order_by is provided then the sort defined in the view will be ignored.

  • {link_row_field}__join
    optional
    string

    Makes it possible to request a lookup of field values from a target table through existing link row fields. The parameter name has to be the name of an existing link row field, followed by __join. The value should be a list of field names for which we want to lookup additional values. You can provide one or multiple target fields. It is not possible to lookup a value of a link row field in the target table.

    With user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter LinkRowField__join=MyField,MyField2 then the values of MyField and My Field2 in the table linked by LinkRowField will be included in the response .

    Without user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter field_1__join=field_2,field_3 then the values of field_2 and field_3 in the table linked by field_1 will be included in the response.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913282/?user_field_names=true
Request sample

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913282/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Get row

Fetch a single Creators row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that is requested.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913282/{row_id}/?user_field_names=true

    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Create row

Create a new Creators row.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before
    optional
    integer
    If provided then the newly created row will be positioned before the row with the provided id.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Name field_7919361
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Topic field_7919373
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.
    5792742
    Comedy
    5792737
    Education
    5792733
    Food
    5792740
    Fun
    5792741
    Gaming
    5792738
    Geek
    5792736
    Kids
    5792739
    People
    5792734
    Science
    5792735
    Tech
  • Real Name field_7919378
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Email field_7919375
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be an email address.
  • YouTube field_7919364
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • followers-YT field_7919367
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a positive decimal with 1 decimal places after the dot.
  • Status field_7919377
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792743
    Inactive
    5792744
    Not suggested
  • Website field_7919374
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Instagram field_7919363
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • followers-Insta field_7919366
    optional
    number
    Accepts a positive number.
  • Facebook field_7919362
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • followers-FB field_7919365
    optional
    number
    Accepts a positive number.
  • views-YT field_7919368
    optional
    number
    Accepts a positive number.
  • TikTok field_7919369
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • followers-TT field_7919370
    optional
    number
    Accepts a positive number.
  • Twitter field_7919371
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • followers-TW field_7919372
    optional
    number
    Accepts a positive number.
  • Articles field_7919376
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913283. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Update row

Updates an existing Creators row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be updated.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Name field_7919361
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Topic field_7919373
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.

    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913283. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Move row

Moves an existing Creators row before another row. If no before_id is provided, then the row will be moved to the end of the table.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    Moves the row related to the value.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before_id
    optional
    integer
    Moves the row related to the given `row_id` before the row related to the provided value. If not provided, then the row will be moved to the end.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
PATCH
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913282/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true

Copied!

  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913282/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Delete row

Deletes an existing Creators row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be deleted.

Query parameters

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
DELETE
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913282/{row_id}/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.delete(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913282/{row_id}/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Articles table

The ID of this table is: 913283

Fields

Each row in the Articles table contains the following fields.

IDNameTypeCompatible filters
field_7919379Linkstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919384Titlestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919380Mediaarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919386Creatorarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913282. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919381Datedatedate_isdate_is_notdate_is_beforedate_is_on_or_beforedate_is_afterdate_is_on_or_afterdate_is_withindate_equaldate_not_equaldate_equals_todaydate_before_todaydate_after_todaydate_within_daysdate_within_weeksdate_within_monthsdate_equals_days_agodate_equals_months_agodate_equals_years_agodate_equals_weekdate_equals_monthdate_equals_yeardate_equals_day_of_monthdate_beforedate_before_or_equaldate_afterdate_after_or_equaldate_after_days_agocontainscontains_notemptynot_empty
Accepts a date time in ISO format.
field_7919382Field of Interestnumbercontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordsingle_select_equalsingle_select_not_equalsingle_select_is_any_ofsingle_select_is_none_ofemptynot_empty
Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
5792745
Hydrogen
5792746
Food Waste
5792747
Metaverse
5792748
Cancer
5792749
Recycling
field_7919383Journalistarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913280. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919385Mentionsarraycontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordmultiple_select_hasmultiple_select_has_notemptynot_empty
Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.
5792750
Clean Hydrogen
5792751
Hydrogen Europe
5792752
Hydrogen Council
5792753
V as in MetaVerse

List fields

To list fields of the Articles table a GET request has to be made to the Articles fields endpoint. It's only possible to list the fields if the database token has read, create or update permissions.

Result field properties

  • id
    integer

    Field primary key. Can be used to generate the database column name by adding field_ prefix.

  • name
    string
    Field name.
  • table_id
    integer
    Related table id.
  • order
    integer
    Field order in table. 0 for the first field.
  • primary
    boolean

    Indicates if the field is a primary field. If true the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

  • type
    string
    Type defined for this field.
  • read_only
    boolean
    Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.
  • description
    string
    Field description

Some extra properties are not described here because they are type specific.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913283/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913283/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
[
    {
        "id": 7919379,
        "table_id": 913283,
        "name": "Link",
        "order": 0,
        "type": "url",
        "primary": true,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919384,
        "table_id": 913283,
        "name": "Title",
        "order": 1,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919380,
        "table_id": 913283,
        "name": "Media",
        "order": 2,
        "type": "link_row",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    }
]

List rows

To list rows in the Articles table a GET request has to be made to the Articles endpoint. The response is paginated and by default the first page is returned. The correct page can be fetched by providing the page and size query parameters.

Query parameters

  • page
    optional
    integer
    Default: 1
    Defines which page of rows should be returned.
  • size
    optional
    integer
    Default: 100
    Defines how many rows should be returned per page.
  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

    Additionally when user_field_names is set then the behaviour of the other GET parameters order_by, include and exclude changes. They instead expect comma separated lists of the actual field names instead.

  • search
    optional
    string
    Default: ''
    If provided only rows with data that matches the search query are going to be returned.
  • order_by
    optional
    string
    Default: 'id'

    Optionally the rows can be ordered by fields separated by comma. By default or if prepended with a '+' a field is ordered in ascending (A-Z) order, but by prepending the field with a '-' it can be ordered descending (Z-A).

    With user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of the field names to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=My Field,-My Field 2 the rows will ordered by the field called My Field in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by the field called My Field 2 in descending order.

    Ensure fields with names starting with a + or - are explicitly prepended with another + or -. E.g +-Name.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=field_1,-field_2 the rows will ordered by field_1 in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by field_2 in descending order.

  • filters
    optional
    JSON

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. This parameter accepts a JSON serialized string containing the filter tree to apply to this view. The filter tree is a nested structure containing the filters that need to be applied.

    With user_field_names:

    An example of a valid filter tree is the following: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": "Name", "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": 1, "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}

    Please note that if this parameter is provided, all other filter__{field}__{filter} will be ignored, as well as the filter_type parameter.

  • filter__{field}__{filter}
    optional
    string

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. Multiple filters may be applied if they follow the same format. The field and filter variables indicate how and where to apply the filter, respectively.

    With user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__Name__equal=test, only rows where the value of Name equals 'test' will be returned. This method is backwards compatible and will check against field_id if it fails on the name.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__field_1__equal=test, only rows where the value of field_1 equals 'test' will be returned.

    Please note that if the filters parameter is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

    A list of all filters can be found here.
  • filter_type
    optional
    string
    Default: 'AND'
    • AND: Indicates that the rows must match all the provided filters.
    • OR: Indicates that the rows only have to match one of the filters.

    This works only if two or more filters are provided.

  • include
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to include by providing the include query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example if you provide the following GET param: include=My Field,-My Field 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter include=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

  • exclude
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to exclude by providing the exclude query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field names to be excluded from the results. For example if you provide the following GET param: exclude=My Field,-My Field 2 then those fields will be excluded.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to exclude from the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter exclude=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 will be excluded.

  • view_id
    optional
    integer

    By default non of the filters and sorts outside of the ones defined in the query parameters are applied. You can add the filters and sorts of a view by providing its id in the view_id GET parameter. For example if you provide the following GET parameter view_id=1 then the filters and sorts defined in the view with id 1 will be applied. You can find the view_id in the context menu of any given view. It is the number in brackets behind the view name.

    With filter__{field}__{filter}

    Both the filter provided in the query parameter and the filters defined in the view will be applied.

    With order_by

    If order_by is provided then the sort defined in the view will be ignored.

  • {link_row_field}__join
    optional
    string

    Makes it possible to request a lookup of field values from a target table through existing link row fields. The parameter name has to be the name of an existing link row field, followed by __join. The value should be a list of field names for which we want to lookup additional values. You can provide one or multiple target fields. It is not possible to lookup a value of a link row field in the target table.

    With user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter LinkRowField__join=MyField,MyField2 then the values of MyField and My Field2 in the table linked by LinkRowField will be included in the response .

    Without user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter field_1__join=field_2,field_3 then the values of field_2 and field_3 in the table linked by field_1 will be included in the response.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913283/?user_field_names=true
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913283/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample

Get row

Fetch a single Articles row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that is requested.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

Create row

Create a new Articles row.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before
    optional
    integer
    If provided then the newly created row will be positioned before the row with the provided id.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Link field_7919379
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Title field_7919384
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Media field_7919380
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Creator field_7919386
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913282. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Date field_7919381
    optional
    date
    Accepts a date time in ISO format.
  • Field of Interest field_7919382
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792745
    Hydrogen
    5792746
    Food Waste
    5792747
    Metaverse
    5792748
    Cancer
    5792749
    Recycling
  • Journalist field_7919383
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913280. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Mentions field_7919385
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.
    5792750
    Clean Hydrogen
    5792751
    Hydrogen Europe
    5792752
    Hydrogen Council
    5792753
    V as in MetaVerse

Update row

Updates an existing Articles row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be updated.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Link field_7919379
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Title field_7919384
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Media field_7919380
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Creator field_7919386
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913282. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Date field_7919381
    optional
    date
    Accepts a date time in ISO format.
  • Field of Interest field_7919382
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792745
    Hydrogen
    5792746
    Food Waste
    5792747
    Metaverse
    5792748
    Cancer
    5792749
    Recycling
  • Journalist field_7919383
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913280. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Mentions field_7919385
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array of mixed integers or text values each representing the chosen select option id or value. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma as value in which case the string is converted into an array of option names.
    5792750
    Clean Hydrogen
    5792751
    Hydrogen Europe
    5792752
    Hydrogen Council
    5792753
    V as in MetaVerse

Move row

Moves an existing Articles row before another row. If no before_id is provided, then the row will be moved to the end of the table.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    Moves the row related to the value.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before_id
    optional
    integer
    Moves the row related to the given `row_id` before the row related to the provided value. If not provided, then the row will be moved to the end.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!

https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913283/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true

)

Delete row

Deletes an existing Articles row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be deleted.

Query parameters

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!

https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913283/{row_id}/

Copied!

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913283/{row_id}/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

ECI Initiatives table

The ID of this table is: 913284

Fields

Each row in the ECI Initiatives table contains the following fields.
