field_7919405ECI Articlesarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913285. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919425Count (Media 2)numberequalnot_equalcontainscontains_nothigher_thanhigher_than_or_equallower_thanlower_than_or_equalis_even_and_wholeemptynot_empty
A read-only field connected to a link to table field which returns the number of relations.

List fields

To list fields of the ECI Initiatives table a GET request has to be made to the ECI Initiatives fields endpoint. It's only possible to list the fields if the database token has read, create or update permissions.

Result field properties

  • id
    integer

    Field primary key. Can be used to generate the database column name by adding field_ prefix.

  • name
    string
    Field name.
  • table_id
    integer
    Related table id.
  • order
    integer
    Field order in table. 0 for the first field.
  • primary
    boolean

    Indicates if the field is a primary field. If true the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

  • type
    string
    Type defined for this field.
  • read_only
    boolean
    Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.
  • description
    string
    Field description

Some extra properties are not described here because they are type specific.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913284/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913284/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
[
    {
        "id": 7919387,
        "table_id": 913284,
        "name": "Πρωτοβουλία",
        "order": 0,
        "type": "long_text",
        "primary": true,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919406,
        "table_id": 913284,
        "name": "English",
        "order": 1,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919388,
        "table_id": 913284,
        "name": "STATUS",
        "order": 2,
        "type": "single_select",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "Έχει δοθεί απάντηση"
    }
]

List rows

To list rows in the ECI Initiatives table a GET request has to be made to the ECI Initiatives endpoint. The response is paginated and by default the first page is returned. The correct page can be fetched by providing the page and size query parameters.

Query parameters

  • page
    optional
    integer
    Default: 1
    Defines which page of rows should be returned.
  • size
    optional
    integer
    Default: 100
    Defines how many rows should be returned per page.
  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

    Additionally when user_field_names is set then the behaviour of the other GET parameters order_by, include and exclude changes. They instead expect comma separated lists of the actual field names instead.

  • search
    optional
    string
    Default: ''
    If provided only rows with data that matches the search query are going to be returned.
  • order_by
    optional
    string
    Default: 'id'

    Optionally the rows can be ordered by fields separated by comma. By default or if prepended with a '+' a field is ordered in ascending (A-Z) order, but by prepending the field with a '-' it can be ordered descending (Z-A).

    With user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of the field names to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=My Field,-My Field 2 the rows will ordered by the field called My Field in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by the field called My Field 2 in descending order.

    Ensure fields with names starting with a + or - are explicitly prepended with another + or -. E.g +-Name.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=field_1,-field_2 the rows will ordered by field_1 in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by field_2 in descending order.

  • filters
    optional
    JSON

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. This parameter accepts a JSON serialized string containing the filter tree to apply to this view. The filter tree is a nested structure containing the filters that need to be applied.

    With user_field_names:

    An example of a valid filter tree is the following: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": "Name", "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": 1, "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}

    Please note that if this parameter is provided, all other filter__{field}__{filter} will be ignored, as well as the filter_type parameter.

  • filter__{field}__{filter}
    optional
    string

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. Multiple filters may be applied if they follow the same format. The field and filter variables indicate how and where to apply the filter, respectively.

    With user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__Name__equal=test, only rows where the value of Name equals 'test' will be returned. This method is backwards compatible and will check against field_id if it fails on the name.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__field_1__equal=test, only rows where the value of field_1 equals 'test' will be returned.

    Please note that if the filters parameter is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

    A list of all filters can be found here.
  • filter_type
    optional
    string
    Default: 'AND'
    • AND: Indicates that the rows must match all the provided filters.
    • OR: Indicates that the rows only have to match one of the filters.

    This works only if two or more filters are provided.

  • include
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to include by providing the include query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example if you provide the following GET param: include=My Field,-My Field 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter include=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

  • exclude
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to exclude by providing the exclude query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field names to be excluded from the results. For example if you provide the following GET param: exclude=My Field,-My Field 2 then those fields will be excluded.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to exclude from the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter exclude=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 will be excluded.

  • view_id
    optional
    integer

    By default non of the filters and sorts outside of the ones defined in the query parameters are applied. You can add the filters and sorts of a view by providing its id in the view_id GET parameter. For example if you provide the following GET parameter view_id=1 then the filters and sorts defined in the view with id 1 will be applied. You can find the view_id in the context menu of any given view. It is the number in brackets behind the view name.

    With filter__{field}__{filter}

    Both the filter provided in the query parameter and the filters defined in the view will be applied.

    With order_by

    If order_by is provided then the sort defined in the view will be ignored.

  • {link_row_field}__join
    optional
    string

    Makes it possible to request a lookup of field values from a target table through existing link row fields. The parameter name has to be the name of an existing link row field, followed by __join. The value should be a list of field names for which we want to lookup additional values. You can provide one or multiple target fields. It is not possible to lookup a value of a link row field in the target table.

    With user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter LinkRowField__join=MyField,MyField2 then the values of MyField and My Field2 in the table linked by LinkRowField will be included in the response .

    Without user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter field_1__join=field_2,field_3 then the values of field_2 and field_3 in the table linked by field_1 will be included in the response.

Copy

GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/?user_field_names=true

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={

    }
)

{
    "count": 1024,
    "next": "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/?page=2",
    "previous": null,
    "results": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "order": "1.00000000000000000000",
            "Πρωτοβουλία": "string",
            "English": "string",
            "STATUS": {
                "id": 1,
                "value": "Option",
                "color": "light-blue"
            },
            "Official Link EN": "https://baserow.io",
            "Official Link GR": "https://baserow.io",

            "Collection Close": "2020-01-01",
            "Valid Initiative": "2020-01-01",
            "Answered Initiative": "2020-01-01",

            "% Sig Point": "0.00",

            "ECI Articles": [
                {
                    "id": 0,
                    "value": "string"
                }
            ],
            "Count (Media 2)": 0
        }
    ]
}

Get row

Fetch a single ECI Initiatives row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that is requested.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/{row_id}/?user_field_names=true
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/{row_id}/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Create row

Create a new ECI Initiatives row.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before
    optional
    integer
    If provided then the newly created row will be positioned before the row with the provided id.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Πρωτοβουλία field_7919387
    optional
    string
    Accepts multi line text. If the rich text formatting is enabled, you can use markdown to format the text.
  • English field_7919406
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • STATUS field_7919388
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792763
    Καταχωρίστηκε
    5792754
    Η συγκέντρωση συνεχίζεται
    5792756
    -
    5792757
    ?
    5792758
    Αποσύρθηκε
    5792759
    Ανεπιτυχής συγκέντρωση
    5792761
    Έγκυρη πρωτοβουλία
    5792760
    Έχει δοθεί απάντηση
    5792762
    Η συγκέντρωση ολοκληρώθηκε
  • Official Link EN field_7919389
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Official Link GR field_7919407
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Registered field_7919390
    optional
    date
    Accepts a date time in ISO format.
  • Collection Close field_7919391
    optional
    date
    Accepts a date time in ISO format.
  • Valid Initiative field_7919408
    optional
    date
    Accepts a date time in ISO format.
  • Answered Initiative field_7919409
    optional
    date
    Accepts a date time in ISO format.
  • Withdrawn field_7919410
    optional
    date
    Accepts a date time in ISO format.
  • Unsuccessful collection field_7919411
    optional
    date
    Accepts a date time in ISO format.
  • Website field_7919392
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • NOTES field_7919393
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Online Signatures field_7919394
    optional
    number
    Accepts a number.
  • % Time Point field_7919395
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a decimal with 2 decimal places after the dot.
  • % Sig Point field_7919396
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a decimal with 2 decimal places after the dot.
  • % Time Left field_7919397
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a decimal with 2 decimal places after the dot.
  • % Sigs Left field_7919398
    optional
    decimal
    Accepts a decimal with 2 decimal places after the dot.
  • FB field_7919399
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • X/T field_7919400
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • INST field_7919401
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • LIN field_7919402
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • BS field_7919403
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • YT field_7919404
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • ECI Articles field_7919405
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913285. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Copied!
POST
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/?user_field_names=true

Update row

Updates an existing ECI Initiatives row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be updated.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Πρωτοβουλία field_7919387
    optional
    string
    Accepts multi line text. If the rich text formatting is enabled, you can use markdown to format the text.
  • English field_7919406
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • STATUS field_7919388
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.

Move row

Moves an existing ECI Initiatives row before another row. If no before_id is provided, then the row will be moved to the end of the table.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    Moves the row related to the value.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before_id
    optional
    integer
    Moves the row related to the given `row_id` before the row related to the provided value. If not provided, then the row will be moved to the end.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
PATCH
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true

  • Python (requests)

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Delete row

Deletes an existing ECI Initiatives row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be deleted.

Query parameters

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
DELETE
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/{row_id}/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.delete(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913284/{row_id}/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

ECI Articles table

The ID of this table is: 913285

Fields

Each row in the ECI Articles table contains the following fields.

IDNameTypeCompatible filters
field_7919412Article Titlestringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts single line text.
field_7919413Linkstringequalnot_equalcontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordlength_is_lower_thanemptynot_empty
Accepts a string that must be a URL.
field_7919418Mediaarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7919414Datedatedate_isdate_is_notdate_is_beforedate_is_on_or_beforedate_is_afterdate_is_on_or_afterdate_is_withindate_equaldate_not_equaldate_equals_todaydate_before_todaydate_after_todaydate_within_daysdate_within_weeksdate_within_monthsdate_equals_days_agodate_equals_months_agodate_equals_years_agodate_equals_weekdate_equals_monthdate_equals_yeardate_equals_day_of_monthdate_beforedate_before_or_equaldate_afterdate_after_or_equaldate_after_days_agocontainscontains_notemptynot_empty
Accepts a date time in ISO format.
field_7919417Promotesnumbercontainscontains_notcontains_worddoesnt_contain_wordsingle_select_equalsingle_select_not_equalsingle_select_is_any_ofsingle_select_is_none_ofemptynot_empty
Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
5792764
ECI
5792765
Initiative
field_7919415Initiativearraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913284. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
field_7936467Journalistsarraylink_row_haslink_row_has_notlink_row_containslink_row_not_containsemptynot_empty
Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913280. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

List fields

To list fields of the ECI Articles table a GET request has to be made to the ECI Articles fields endpoint. It's only possible to list the fields if the database token has read, create or update permissions.

Result field properties

  • id
    integer

    Field primary key. Can be used to generate the database column name by adding field_ prefix.

  • name
    string
    Field name.
  • table_id
    integer
    Related table id.
  • order
    integer
    Field order in table. 0 for the first field.
  • primary
    boolean

    Indicates if the field is a primary field. If true the field cannot be deleted and the value should represent the whole row.

  • type
    string
    Type defined for this field.
  • read_only
    boolean
    Indicates whether the field is a read only field. If true, it's not possible to update the cell value.
  • description
    string
    Field description

Some extra properties are not described here because they are type specific.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913285/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/fields/table/913285/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
[
    {
        "id": 7919412,
        "table_id": 913285,
        "name": "Article Title",
        "order": 0,
        "type": "text",
        "primary": true,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919413,
        "table_id": 913285,
        "name": "Link",
        "order": 1,
        "type": "url",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    },
    {
        "id": 7919418,
        "table_id": 913285,
        "name": "Media",
        "order": 2,
        "type": "link_row",
        "primary": false,
        "read_only": false,
        "description": "A sample description"
    }
]

List rows

To list rows in the ECI Articles table a GET request has to be made to the ECI Articles endpoint. The response is paginated and by default the first page is returned. The correct page can be fetched by providing the page and size query parameters.

Query parameters

  • page
    optional
    integer
    Default: 1
    Defines which page of rows should be returned.
  • size
    optional
    integer
    Default: 100
    Defines how many rows should be returned per page.
  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

    Additionally when user_field_names is set then the behaviour of the other GET parameters order_by, include and exclude changes. They instead expect comma separated lists of the actual field names instead.

  • search
    optional
    string
    Default: ''
    If provided only rows with data that matches the search query are going to be returned.
  • order_by
    optional
    string
    Default: 'id'

    Optionally the rows can be ordered by fields separated by comma. By default or if prepended with a '+' a field is ordered in ascending (A-Z) order, but by prepending the field with a '-' it can be ordered descending (Z-A).

    With user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of the field names to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=My Field,-My Field 2 the rows will ordered by the field called My Field in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by the field called My Field 2 in descending order.

    Ensure fields with names starting with a + or - are explicitly prepended with another + or -. E.g +-Name.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    order_by should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to order by. For example if you provide the following GET parameter order_by=field_1,-field_2 the rows will ordered by field_1 in ascending order. If some fields have the same value, that subset will be ordered by field_2 in descending order.

  • filters
    optional
    JSON

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. This parameter accepts a JSON serialized string containing the filter tree to apply to this view. The filter tree is a nested structure containing the filters that need to be applied.

    With user_field_names:

    An example of a valid filter tree is the following: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": "Name", "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: {"filter_type": "AND", "filters": [{"field": 1, "type": "equal", "value": "test"}]}

    Please note that if this parameter is provided, all other filter__{field}__{filter} will be ignored, as well as the filter_type parameter.

  • filter__{field}__{filter}
    optional
    string

    Rows can optionally be filtered using the same view filters that are available for the views. Multiple filters may be applied if they follow the same format. The field and filter variables indicate how and where to apply the filter, respectively.

    With user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__Name__equal=test, only rows where the value of Name equals 'test' will be returned. This method is backwards compatible and will check against field_id if it fails on the name.

    Without user_field_names:

    For example, if you optionally provide the following GET parameter: filter__field_1__equal=test, only rows where the value of field_1 equals 'test' will be returned.

    Please note that if the filters parameter is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

    A list of all filters can be found here.
  • filter_type
    optional
    string
    Default: 'AND'
    • AND: Indicates that the rows must match all the provided filters.
    • OR: Indicates that the rows only have to match one of the filters.

    This works only if two or more filters are provided.

  • include
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to include by providing the include query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example if you provide the following GET param: include=My Field,-My Field 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    include should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter include=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 then only those fields will be included (unless they are explicitly excluded).

  • exclude
    optional
    string

    All the fields are included in the response by default. You can select a subset of fields to exclude by providing the exclude query parameter.

    With user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field names to be excluded from the results. For example if you provide the following GET param: exclude=My Field,-My Field 2 then those fields will be excluded.

    The name of fields containing commas must be surrounded by quotes: "Name ,". If the field names contain quotes, then they must be escaped using the \ character. Eg: Name \".

    Without user_field_names:

    exclude should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to exclude from the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter exclude=field_1,field_2 then the fields with id 1 and id 2 will be excluded.

  • view_id
    optional
    integer

    By default non of the filters and sorts outside of the ones defined in the query parameters are applied. You can add the filters and sorts of a view by providing its id in the view_id GET parameter. For example if you provide the following GET parameter view_id=1 then the filters and sorts defined in the view with id 1 will be applied. You can find the view_id in the context menu of any given view. It is the number in brackets behind the view name.

    With filter__{field}__{filter}

    Both the filter provided in the query parameter and the filters defined in the view will be applied.

    With order_by

    If order_by is provided then the sort defined in the view will be ignored.

  • {link_row_field}__join
    optional
    string

    Makes it possible to request a lookup of field values from a target table through existing link row fields. The parameter name has to be the name of an existing link row field, followed by __join. The value should be a list of field names for which we want to lookup additional values. You can provide one or multiple target fields. It is not possible to lookup a value of a link row field in the target table.

    With user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field names to be included in results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter LinkRowField__join=MyField,MyField2 then the values of MyField and My Field2 in the table linked by LinkRowField will be included in the response .

    Without user_field_names:

    join should be a comma separated list of field_ followed by the id of the field to include in the results. For example: If you provide the following GET parameter field_1__join=field_2,field_3 then the values of field_2 and field_3 in the table linked by field_1 will be included in the response.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913285/?user_field_names=true
Request sample

    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913285/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Get row

Fetch a single ECI Articles row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that is requested.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

Create row

Create a new ECI Articles row.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before
    optional
    integer
    If provided then the newly created row will be positioned before the row with the provided id.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Request body schema

  • Article Title field_7919412
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Link field_7919413
    optional
    string
    Accepts a string that must be a URL.
  • Media field_7919418
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Date field_7919414
    optional
    date
    Accepts a date time in ISO format.
  • Promotes field_7919417
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.
    5792764
    ECI
    5792765
    Initiative
  • Initiative field_7919415
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913284. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Journalists field_7936467
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913280. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Update row

Updates an existing ECI Articles row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be updated.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

  • Article Title field_7919412
    optional
    string
    Accepts single line text.
  • Link field_7919413

  • Media field_7919418
    optional
    array
    Accepts an array containing the identifiers or main field text values of the related rows from table id 913278. All identifiers must be provided every time the relations are updated. If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.
  • Date field_7919414
    optional
    date

  • Promotes field_7919417
    optional
    number
    Accepts an integer or a text value representing the chosen select option id or option value. A null value means none is selected. In case of a text value, the first matching option is selected.

You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

If an empty array is provided all relations will be deleted. In case of a text value instead of an identifier, a row with the matching value for its main field will be searched. If more than one match is found, the first one in the order of the table is selected. You can send a string with names separated by comma in which case the string is converted into an array of row names. You can also send only an ID of a row alone.

Move row

Moves an existing ECI Articles row before another row. If no before_id is provided, then the row will be moved to the end of the table.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    Moves the row related to the value.

Query parameters

  • user_field_names
    optional
    any

    When the user_field_names GET parameter is provided and its value is one of the following: y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or empty string, the field names returned by this endpoint will be the actual names of the fields.

    If the user_field_names GET parameter is not provided, or if it does not match any of the above values, then all returned field names will be field_ followed by the id of the field. For example field_1 refers to the field with an id of 1.

  • before_id
    optional
    integer
    Moves the row related to the given `row_id` before the row related to the provided value. If not provided, then the row will be moved to the end.
  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
PATCH
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913285/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.patch(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913285/{row_id}/move/?user_field_names=true",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
field_7919412
field_7919413
field_7919418
field_7919414
field_7919417
field_7919415
field_7936467
{
    "id": 0,
    "order": "1.00000000000000000000",
    "Article Title": "string",
    "Link": "https://baserow.io",
    "Media": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "value": "string"
        }
    ],
    "Date": "2020-01-01",
    "Promotes": {
        "id": 1,
        "value": "Option",
        "color": "light-blue"
    },
    "Initiative": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "value": "string"
        }
    ],
    "Journalists": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "value": "string"
        }
    ]
}

Delete row

Deletes an existing ECI Articles row.

Path parameters

  • row_id
    integer
    The unique identifier of the row that needs to be deleted.

Query parameters

  • send_webhook_events
    optional
    any

    A flag query parameter that triggers webhooks after the operation, if set to y, yes, true, t, on, 1, or left empty. Defaults to true

Copy
Copied!
DELETE
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913285/{row_id}/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.delete(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/rows/table/913285/{row_id}/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)

Upload file

Uploads a file to Baserow by uploading the file contents directly. A file multipart is expected containing the file contents. The response can then be used to upload a file to a row.

Request body schema

  • file
    multipart

    The file multipart containing the file contents.

Copy
Copied!
POST
https://api.baserow.io/api/user-files/upload-file/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.post(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/user-files/upload-file/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
    files={'file': open('photo.png', 'rb')}
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
{
    "url": "https://files.baserow.io/user_files/VXotniBOVm8tbstZkKsMKbj2Qg7KmPvn_39d354a76abe56baaf569ad87d0333f58ee4bf3eed368e3b9dc736fd18b09dfd.png",
    "thumbnails": {
        "tiny": {
            "url": "https://files.baserow.io/media/thumbnails/tiny/VXotniBOVm8tbstZkKsMKbj2Qg7KmPvn_39d354a76abe56baaf569ad87d0333f58ee4bf3eed368e3b9dc736fd18b09dfd.png",
            "width": 21,
            "height": 21
        },
        "small": {
            "url": "https://files.baserow.io/media/thumbnails/small/VXotniBOVm8tbstZkKsMKbj2Qg7KmPvn_39d354a76abe56baaf569ad87d0333f58ee4bf3eed368e3b9dc736fd18b09dfd.png",
            "width": 48,
            "height": 48
        }
    },
    "name": "VXotniBOVm8tbstZkKsMKbj2Qg7KmPvn_39d354a76abe56baaf569ad87d0333f58ee4bf3eed368e3b9dc736fd18b09dfd.png",
    "size": 229940,
    "mime_type": "image/png",
    "is_image": true,
    "image_width": 1280,
    "image_height": 585,
    "uploaded_at": "2020-11-17T12:16:10.035234+00:00"
}

List all tables

This endpoint only works in combination with the token authentication. It lists all the tables that the token has either create, read, update or delete access to.

Copy
Copied!
GET
https://api.baserow.io/api/database/tables/all-tables/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/database/tables/all-tables/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
[
    {
        "id": 0,
        "name": "string",
        "order": 2147483647,
        "database_id": 0
    }
]

Upload file via URL

Uploads a file to Baserow by downloading it from the provided URL. The response can then be used to upload a file to a row.

Request body schema

  • url
    string

    Uploads a file to Baserow by downloading it from the provided URL.

Copy
Copied!
POST
https://api.baserow.io/api/user-files/upload-via-url/
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.post(
    "https://api.baserow.io/api/user-files/upload-via-url/",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    },
    json={
        "url": "https://baserow.io/assets/photo.png"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
{
    "url": "https://files.baserow.io/user_files/VXotniBOVm8tbstZkKsMKbj2Qg7KmPvn_39d354a76abe56baaf569ad87d0333f58ee4bf3eed368e3b9dc736fd18b09dfd.png",
    "thumbnails": {
        "tiny": {
            "url": "https://files.baserow.io/media/thumbnails/tiny/VXotniBOVm8tbstZkKsMKbj2Qg7KmPvn_39d354a76abe56baaf569ad87d0333f58ee4bf3eed368e3b9dc736fd18b09dfd.png",
            "width": 21,
            "height": 21
        },
        "small": {
            "url": "https://files.baserow.io/media/thumbnails/small/VXotniBOVm8tbstZkKsMKbj2Qg7KmPvn_39d354a76abe56baaf569ad87d0333f58ee4bf3eed368e3b9dc736fd18b09dfd.png",
            "width": 48,
            "height": 48
        }
    },
    "name": "VXotniBOVm8tbstZkKsMKbj2Qg7KmPvn_39d354a76abe56baaf569ad87d0333f58ee4bf3eed368e3b9dc736fd18b09dfd.png",
    "size": 229940,
    "mime_type": "image/png",
    "is_image": true,
    "image_width": 1280,
    "image_height": 585,
    "uploaded_at": "2020-11-17T12:16:10.035234+00:00"
}

Filters

FilterExample valueFull example
equal stringfield is 'string'
not_equal stringfield is not 'string'
date_is UTC??todayfield is 'UTC??today'
date_is_not UTC??todayfield is not 'UTC??today'
date_is_before UTC??todayfield is before 'UTC??today'
date_is_on_or_before UTC??todayfield is on or before 'UTC??today'
date_is_after UTC??todayfield is after 'UTC??today'
date_is_on_or_after UTC??todayfield is on or after 'UTC??today'
date_is_within UTC??todayfield is within 'UTC??today'
date_equal
deprecated
2020-01-01field is date '2020-01-01'
date_not_equal
deprecated
2020-01-01field is not date '2020-01-01'
date_equals_today
deprecated
UTCfield is today 'UTC'
date_before_today
deprecated
UTCfield is before today 'UTC'
date_after_today
deprecated
UTCfield is after today 'UTC'
date_within_days
deprecated
Europe/Athens?1field is within days 'Europe/Athens?1'
date_within_weeks
deprecated
Europe/Athens?1field is within weeks 'Europe/Athens?1'
date_within_months
deprecated
Europe/Athens?1field is within months 'Europe/Athens?1'
date_equals_days_ago
deprecated
Europe/Athens?1field is days ago 'Europe/Athens?1'
date_equals_months_ago
deprecated
Europe/Athens?1field is months ago 'Europe/Athens?1'
date_equals_years_ago
deprecated
Europe/Athens?1field is years ago 'Europe/Athens?1'
date_equals_week
deprecated
UTCfield in this week 'UTC'
date_equals_month
deprecated
UTCfield in this month 'UTC'
date_equals_year
deprecated
UTCfield in this year 'UTC'
date_equals_day_of_month 1field day of month is '1'
date_before
deprecated
2020-01-01field is before date '2020-01-01'
date_before_or_equal
deprecated
2020-01-01field is before or same date '2020-01-01'
date_after
deprecated
2020-01-01field is after date '2020-01-01'
date_after_or_equal
deprecated
2020-01-01field is after or same date '2020-01-01'
date_after_days_ago
deprecated
20field is after days ago '20'
has_empty_value stringfield has empty value 'string'
has_not_empty_value stringfield doesn't have empty value 'string'
has_value_equal stringfield has value equal 'string'
has_not_value_equal stringfield doesn't have value equal 'string'
has_value_contains stringfield has value contains 'string'
has_not_value_contains stringfield doesn't have value contains 'string'
has_value_contains_word stringfield has value contains word 'string'
has_not_value_contains_word stringfield doesn't have value contains word 'string'
has_value_length_is_lower_than stringfield has value length is lower than 'string'
has_all_values_equal stringfield has all values equal 'string'
has_any_select_option_equal stringfield has any select option equal 'string'
has_none_select_option_equal stringfield doesn't have select option equal 'string'
contains stringfield contains 'string'
contains_not stringfield doesn't contain 'string'
contains_word stringfield contains word 'string'
doesnt_contain_word stringfield doesn't contain word 'string'
filename_contains stringfield filename contains 'string'
has_file_type image | documentfield has file type 'image | document'
files_lower_than 2field files lower than '2'
length_is_lower_than 5field length is lower than '5'
higher_than 100field higher than '100'
higher_than_or_equal 100field higher than or equal '100'
lower_than 100field lower than '100'
lower_than_or_equal 100field lower than or equal '100'
is_even_and_whole truefield is even and whole 'true'
single_select_equal 1field is '1'
single_select_not_equal 1field is not '1'
single_select_is_any_of 1,2field is any of '1,2'
single_select_is_none_of 1,2field is none of '1,2'
boolean truefield is 'true'
link_row_has 1field has '1'
link_row_has_not 1field doesn't have '1'
link_row_contains stringfield contains 'string'
link_row_not_contains stringfield doesn't contain 'string'
multiple_select_has 1field has any of '1'
multiple_select_has_not 1field doesn't have any of '1'
multiple_collaborators_has 1field has '1'
multiple_collaborators_has_not 1field doesn't have '1'
empty field is empty
not_empty field is not empty
user_is 1field is '1'
user_is_not 1field is not '1'
has_value_higher stringfield has value higher than 'string'
has_not_value_higher stringfield doesn't have value higher than 'string'
has_value_higher_or_equal stringfield has value higher than or equal 'string'
has_not_value_higher_or_equal stringfield doesn't have value higher than or equal 'string'
has_value_lower stringfield has value lower than 'string'
has_not_value_lower stringfield doesn't have value lower than 'string'
has_value_lower_or_equal stringfield has value lower than or equal 'string'
has_not_value_lower_or_equal stringfield doesn't have value lower than or equal 'string'
has_date_equal UTC??todayfield has date equal 'UTC??today'
has_not_date_equal UTC??todayfield doesn't have date equal 'UTC??today'
has_date_before UTC??todayfield has date before 'UTC??today'
has_not_date_before UTC??todayfield doesn't have date before 'UTC??today'
has_date_on_or_before UTC??todayfield has date on or before 'UTC??today'
has_not_date_on_or_before UTC??todayfield doesn't have date on or before 'UTC??today'
has_date_after UTC??todayfield has date after 'UTC??today'
has_not_date_after UTC??todayfield doesn't have date after 'UTC??today'
has_date_on_or_after UTC??todayfield has date on or after 'UTC??today'
has_not_date_on_or_after UTC??todayfield doesn't have date on or after 'UTC??today'
has_date_within UTC??todayfield has date within 'UTC??today'
has_not_date_within UTC??todayfield doesn't have date within 'UTC??today'

HTTP Errors

Error codeNameDescription
200OkRequest completed successfully.
400Bad requestThe request contains invalid values or the JSON could not be parsed.
401UnauthorizedWhen you try to access an endpoint without a valid database token.
404Not foundRow or table is not found.
413Request Entity Too LargeThe request exceeded the maximum allowed payload size.
500Internal Server ErrorThe server encountered an unexpected condition.
502Bad gatewayBaserow is restarting or an unexpected outage is in progress.
503Service unavailableThe server could not process your request in time.
Request sample
Copy
Copied!
Python (requests)
  • cURL
  • HTTP
  • JavaScript (axios)
  • Python (requests)
requests.get(
    "https://api.baserow.io",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Token YOUR_DATABASE_TOKEN"
    }
)
Response sample
Copy
Copied!
{
    "error": "ERROR_NO_PERMISSION_TO_TABLE",
    "description": "The token does not have permissions to the table."
}
